title: Create on_auth_user_created trigger
description: Create a Postgres trigger that automatically inserts a profiles row whenever a new user is created in auth.users. This ensures every auth user always has a corresponding profile.
acceptance_criteria: A trigger function `handle_new_user` exists in the public schema
The trigger fires AFTER INSERT ON auth.users FOR EACH ROW
It inserts a row into public.profiles with id = NEW.id
display_name is populated from NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>'full_name' when present, else empty string
wallet_address is populated from NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>'wallet_address' when present, else NULL
Inserting a test user into auth.users results in a matching profiles row

steps.description: Verify by creating a test user
steps.details: In Supabase Auth dashboard → Users → Invite user (test@example.com). Then run: SELECT * FROM profiles WHERE id = (SELECT id FROM auth.users WHERE email = 'test@example.com'); — confirm a profile row exists.

technical_notes: SECURITY DEFINER is required so the trigger can write to public.profiles even when called in the auth schema context. The function runs as the function owner (postgres), not the calling user.
